text: L-Threonine, chemical formula C4H9NO3, molecular weight 119.12, is a pure organic molecular crystal amino acid polymorph I. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group P212121 (No. 19), with unit cell a = 13.628 Å, b = 7.618 Å, c = 5.110 Å, Z = 4, volume 530.51 Å³, determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 12 K with an R factor of 2.60% (CCDC 129494, CSD refcode LTHREO03). The crystal exhibits piezoelectric behavior with a piezoelectric coefficient d33 of approximately 1.92 pC/N for pure L-threonine and 2.76 pC/N for methylene-blue-doped L-threonine. Recent comprehensive characterization using multiple techniques including FTIR, UV-vis, PXRD, SEM-EDX, and nonlinear optical studies revealed enhanced optical, mechanical, and thermal properties in dye-doped specimens. The dye-doped crystals demonstrated superior second harmonic generation efficiency of 2.2 times that of KDP, enhanced laser damage threshold of 33.4 mJ cm⁻², improved mechanical hardness, and third-order nonlinear optical susceptibility of 8.3452 × 10⁻⁵ esu, making L-threonine promising for nonlinear optical applications, optical modulators, photonic devices, and piezoelectric sensors.
property_ref_title: An exploration of enhanced characteristics and efficiency of dye-doped L-Threonine single crystal
